task_completion.description: Log in to the Rancher UI
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rancher dashboard is visible, indicating successful login using the provided admin credentials.

task_completion.description: Locate the admission webhooks in the cluster
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rancher UI shows the AdmissionRegistration section (MutatingWebhookConfigurations or ValidatingWebhookConfigurations) or a terminal shows a 'kubectl get' command for webhooks.

task_completion.description: Delete or disable the broken/orphaned admission webhooks
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the deletion of the problematic webhooks in the Rancher UI (e.g., clicking delete on a webhook) or a terminal showing a successful 'kubectl delete' command for the webhooks.

task_completion.description: Create the 'webhook-test' deployment with the specified configuration
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rancher workload creation screen or a terminal command showing the creation of a deployment named 'webhook-test' in the 'staging' namespace, using the 'nginx:alpine' image with 1 replica.

task_completion.description: Verify the deployment successfully runs, proving the blockage is resolved
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rancher workloads list shows 'webhook-test' in the 'staging' namespace with a green 'Active' or 'Running' status, or a terminal shows 'kubectl get pods -n staging' with the pod in a 'Running' state.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual Rancher UI or Kubernetes cluster
integrity.visual_evidence: Actions are performed in the web browser (Rancher) or a real terminal emulator, not mocked in a text editor or notes app.

integrity.description: Agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the deployment status
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser developer tools (F12) are not open to manipulate the DOM to show an 'Active' status for the deployment.

integrity.description: Agent did not manually type out a fake success message
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal outputs or UI states are genuine system responses, not manually typed text echoing the expected success state.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target namespace: 'staging'. Test deployment name: 'webhook-test'. Test image: 'nginx:alpine'. The task requires the agent to resolve broken admission webhooks and successfully deploy the test deployment in the staging namespace to prove the blockage is removed. A running 'webhook-test' pod in 'staging' is the ultimate proof of success.
